amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Biofilm formation in Bacillus subtilis is a multicellular developmental program where cells transition from motile planktonic to sessile matrix-producing state. The master regulator SinR represses biofilm genes under planktonic conditions. Under biofilm-inducing conditions, Spo0A~P and multiple signals activate sinI (SinR antagonist) and matrix genes. The EPS biosynthesis (epsA-O) and TasA amyloid fiber (tasA, tapA, sipW) operons produce extracellular matrix. The transition involves multiple bistable switches, quorum sensing, and coordination with motility repression.

name: Biofilm Formation and Matrix Production
notes: Biofilm formation shows very high OR gates (10) for multiple inducing signals. Demonstrates bacterial developmental decision with bistable switch.
scientificAccuracy: Based on extensive research on B. subtilis biofilm formation. All components validated.

sources.title: Cell development: social behavior in a bacterium
Amyloid fibers provide structural integrity to Bacillus subtilis biofilms
sources.authors: Romero D, Aguilar C, Losick R, Kolter R.
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

sources.title: The genetic architecture of biofilm formation in Bacillus subtilis
